questions.question: Explain the fundamental problem that Locally Weighted Regression (LWR) is designed to solve, and state its core assumption.
keyPoints: LWR solves the problem of underfitting complex, non-linear data that global linear models struggle with.
It assumes that the function we are trying to model is smooth and can be approximated linearly within a small, local region around any query point.

questions.question: Describe the process of making a prediction for a single query point using Locally Weighted Regression. What is the role and a common example of the kernel function?
keyPoints: For a given query point x_q, a new local model (e.g., linear regression) is fit using the entire training dataset.
Each training example is weighted by its proximity to x_q using a kernel function (e.g., the Gaussian kernel).
Points closer to x_q have a higher weight and influence the local model more than distant points.
The prediction for x_q is made by evaluating this newly fitted, locally weighted model at x_q.

questions.question: Compare and contrast standard linear regression and Locally Weighted Regression (LWR). Discuss their key characteristics, advantages, disadvantages, and the primary computational cost associated with LWR.
keyPoints: Standard Linear Regression: A global model. Fit once on the entire dataset. Low computational cost (O(n) for prediction after training). Prone to underfitting complex data.
Locally Weighted Regression: A local, instance-based method. A new model is fit for every query point. Highly flexible and can model complex, non-linear functions. No explicit training phase.
Primary Advantage of LWR: Its non-parametric flexibility allows it to model highly complex relationships without specifying a global functional form.

Primary Disadvantage/Computational Cost of LWR: It is computationally expensive for prediction (O(n) per query point) because fitting a new weighted regression is required for every single prediction. This makes it unsuitable for real-time applications or very large datasets.
